headline: Cypress Creek acquires 2.4GW solar, 720MW BESS project in Arkansas from Swift Current
summary_80w: Cypress Creek Renewables has acquired the Steel River project in Arkansas from Swift Current Energy, which will come online in 2029.

headline: Foresight acquires 300MW New Zealand solar-plus-storage platform via AU$500 million invetsment
summary_80w: Foresight Group has made its first investment in New Zealand's renewable energy sector, acquiring development platform NZ Clean Energy (NZCE).

headline: What makes a ‘priority market’ for scaling long-duration energy storage?
summary_80w: The LDES Council’s policy team discusses the role of policy and markets in scaling long-duration energy storage globally with ESN Premium.

headline: Swift Solar’s Meyer Burger asset purchase is ‘pivotal moment’ for US solar manufacturing
summary_80w: PV Talk: 'The US is entering a pivotal moment for domestic solar manufacturing,' Swift Solar CEO Joel Jean told PV Tech Premium this week.

headline: Primergy closes US$760 million refinancing for Gemini solar-plus-storage project
summary_80w: Primergy, launched by Quinbrook Infrastructure Partners, has secured a US$760 million refinancing for its Gemini Solar and Storage Project in Clark County, Nevada.

headline: Swift Solar acquires Meyer Burger’s US HJT manufacturing assets and patents
summary_80w: Perovskite-silicon tandem cell manufacturer Swift Solar has acquired manufacturing assets formerly belonging to Meyer Burger.

headline: Google finalises Intersect acquisition to bring data centre power in-house
summary_80w: Google has finalised its acquisition of US renewable energy developer Intersect Power, a part of its plan to power its data centre expansions in the US.

headline: Middle East energy shock prompts industry call for renewables acceleration

headline: Top ten solar manufacturers shipped over 500GW of modules in 2025

headline: Australia’s AEMC proposes new standards to prevent data centre blackouts amid rising energy demand
summary_80w: The AEMC has released draft technical standards requiring large data centres to remain connected during grid faults.

headline: New Jersey state government advances 1GW of battery storage in energy affordability push

headline: US BESS industry is ‘primed for years of growth’: Enertis Applus+ on financing storage in the FEOC era

headline: US Roundup: Over US$2 billion of financing updates from Arevon, Primergy and Gridstor
summary_80w: In this US news roundup, we have financing updates for GridStor, Arevon, and Primergy, related to energy storage projects in Texas, California, and Nevada, respectively.

summary_80w: Another busy week of BESS news from across Europe, with investors and owner-operators ABO, Field, Aspiravi, Prime Capital, Latvenergo, Sonnedix and Amarenco progressing large-scale projects across Europe.

headline: AGL’s Torrens Island BESS in South Australia hit by a second AEMO direction

headline: EU awards record 25.2GW of new solar PV capacity through government auctions in 2025
summary_80w: EU member states awarded a record 25.2GW of new solar PV capacity through auctions in 2025, according to SolarPower Europe.

headline: California rooftop solar receives setback as court upholds NEM3.0
summary_80w: The California Court of Appeals has upheld the state's ongoing net energy metering programme, NEM3.0, dealing a setback to rooftop solar.

headline: VDE highlights underestimated wind speed factor in hail damage to PV assets

summary_80w: The selling price of several solar PV module technology types in Europe has increased between January and February of this year.

headline: Beazley acquires kWh Analytics

headline: Rethinking the next wave of TOPCon production

headline: BESS asset operation in CAISO is complex, but lean teams can leverage advanced analytics

summary_80w: Recently, utility Xcel Energy announced it would install 30GWh of US startup Form Energy’s iron-air batteries at a data centre in Pine Island, Minnesota, owned by tech giant Google.

headline: FREE DOWNLOAD: The Energy Storage Report 2026

headline: US adds 43.2GW of new solar PV capacity in 2025

headline: Polysilicon prices plunge, with maximum decline exceeding 12%

headline: Policy and regulation risks are a common theme in Europe and US energy storage markets

headline: Why the Middle East crisis could prompt a doubling down on solar

summary_80w: PPA prices are set to continue to rise in the short-term, “as demand outpaces supply”, accotding to Geoffrey Lehv of kWh Analytics.

headline: New providers, new structures drive evolution of BESS offtake in Europe

All in all, the projects total at least 3.7GWh of capacity.

headline: Chinese energy storage players’ 2025 earnings forecasts: High growth, turnarounds and profit declines
